spec_match.rationale: The visualizer now targets actual processing-instruction ranges in a streamed dashboard document.

interactive.rationale: The slow-target controls change the server stream, but browser patch behavior still needs chrome-devtools-mcp verification.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page describes configurable <template for> patch targets rather than synthetic chunk dispatch.

edge_cases.rationale: The route lets any of the hero, stats, or activity ranges become the delayed patch while the others stream first.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus and the WICG patching explainer.

design_system.rationale: The rebuilt page uses the shared live-patching styles and design tokens.

openQuestions.title: Verify native parser patching in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp to switch slow targets and confirm the iframe patches the other ranges first without client DOM replacement.

summary: Converted the visualizer from simulated dispatch to real configurable server-streamed parser patches.
